sourceMovie.title: The Flu
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Flu (2013) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Kim Sung-soo's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Escape from New York
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Flu clicked for you, Escape from New York (1981) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Flu?
faq.answer: Try Escape from New York, The Omega Man, I Am Legend—each shares DNA with The Flu while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Flu?
faq.answer: The Flu (2013) is best described as Action, Science Fiction, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Flu worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
